entry.title: 🇨🇭 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Bern, Switzerland)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud Development Model in SAP S/4HANA 2022.  

We will give you an introduction about the ABAP Cloud Development in an SAP S/4HANA 2022 on-premise environment. The fundamental principle is that ABAP cloud-ready and ABAP classic code can coexist in the same system since the ABAP language version is defined on ABAP object level. This offers a gradual transition from the classic to the cloud extensibility model.

To understand the basic idea of ABAP Cloud Development you will learn how to:

- Use the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) to develop transactional Fiori applications

- Use public SAP APIs released by SAP to access SAP functionality and SAP data

- Use public SAP extension points released by SAP to extend SAP objects

- Use ADT (ABAP Development Tools) as your ABAP IDE

With the SAP S/4HANA 2022 release the same extensibility model can now be used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday 31 March 2023 in Bern, Switzerland from 13:00 - 18:00 CET. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Address:

Post Schweiz AG

RES Wank4 1.08 Konferenz, Meetingroom

Wankdorfallee 4

3030 Bern

The instructor is Ingo Braeuninger

The event is kindly being hosted by Post Schweiz AG, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. 

Please find the prerequisites for this event here.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: 🇵🇱 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Wroclaw, PL)
entry.content:
Note: Registration is now closed as we've reached the physical capacity of the event. The RSVP option is still available, but anyone that RSVPs after today (May 19th, 2023) does not necessarily have a seat.

This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud Development Model in SAP S/4HANA 2022.  

We will give yo an introduction about the ABAP Cloud Development in an SAP S/4HANA 2022 on-premise environment. The fundamental principle is that ABAP cloud-ready and ABAP classic code can coexist in the same system since the ABAP language version is defined on ABAP object level. This offers a gradual transition from the classic to the cloud extensibility model.

To understand the basic idea of ABAP Cloud Development you will learn how to:

- Use the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) to develop transactional Fiori applications

- Use public SAP APIs released by SAP to access SAP functionality and SAP data

- Use public SAP extension points released by SAP to extend SAP objects

- Use ADT (ABAP Development Tools) as your ABAP IDE

With the SAP S/4HANA 2022 release the same extensibility model can now be used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday 23 June 2023 in Wroclaw, Poland from 9:00 - 16:00 CET. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Address: Capgemini Offices,  ul. Legnicka 48H (Business Garden), 54-202 Wrocław, Poland

The instructor is Ingo Braeuninger

The event is kindly being hosted by Capgemini, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. 

Please find the prerequisites for this event here.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: 🇺🇸 SAP CodeJam RAP vs CAP (Dallas, TX, USA)
entry.content:
Note: Registration is now closed as we've reached the physical capacity of the event. The RSVP option is still available, but anyone that RSVPs after today (June 14th, 2023) does not necessarily have a seat.

 

For this SAP CodeJam event, we would like to try something different and offer a hybrid topic SAP CodeJam.  The idea is to merge the RAP and the CAP CodeJam offerings into one event, allowing attendees to get a taste for both the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model(RAP), and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model(CAP) with SAP HANA.  The instructors will present an overview of both programming models where attendees can draw comparisons between them.  Attendees will have the choice of which exercise set they want to work on during the event, and can of course choose to do the remaining exercises on their own later at home.  

Topic abstracts:

ABAP Cloud & the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model -  Attendees will be introduced  to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment. Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud to Create Full-Stack Applications. Attendees will learn how to deploy and configure an instance of the SAP HANA Cloud trial, develop a multi-target application using SAP Business Application Studio and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, and create a service layer and SAP Fiori UI that also includes SAP HANA native artifacts, such as calculation views.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday 16 June 2023 in Dallas, TX, USA from 09:00 - 16:00 CDT. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Address:

SAP America

7500 Windrose Avenue
Suite 250
Plano, TX  75024

The instructors are Developer Advocates Rich Heilman and Thomas Jung. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location.

If you are interested in the ABAP Cloud & ABAP RESTful Application Programming model, please check the prerequisites here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

If you are interested in the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud to Create Full-Stack Applications, please check the prerequisites here: cap-hana-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/cap-hana-exercises-codejam (github....

 Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. 

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Code Challenge – Open-Source ABAP (Week 2)
entry.content:
It should install into the ABAP trial systems, it could also already be installed there from someone else as well.  If so, you only have to create an application using it and you can skip the install step.

Cheers,

Rich Heilman

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Code Challenge – Open-Source ABAP (Week 2)
entry.content: Ok, I can confirm that ABAP2UI5 is installed on the trial instance  https://c75f608d-4148-434a-88e1-64b75d24911a.abap-web.us10.hana.ondemand.com/    So if you are on that trial instance, you need not install it, just use the ABAP2UI5 framework to create your app as outlined above.

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Code Challenge – ABAP Mustache (Week 3)
entry.content:
Tears for Fears and A-Ha are two of my favorites from the 80s.

Cheers,

Rich

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Code Challenge – ABAP Mustache (Week 3)
entry.content: Ah, you're killin me bro!  LOL

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Code Challenge – ABAP Mustache (Week 3)
entry.content: Awesome....  🙂

entry.title: Re: Global Running Day with SAP Community
entry.content:
Hello from York, PA, USA!   Its a little smokey around these parts due to wildfires in Canada, but ran outside anyway.  Just a short one for me today as it is my last run before race day.  Nice and easy....  there's not too many things better than the training taper.  🙂

Congrats to all who got out there and got it done!   #GlobalRunningDay

 

Cheers,

Rich

entry.title: SAP Developer News, June 22nd, 2023
entry.content:
Survey About the SAP BTP Command Line Interface (btp CLI) 

  • Survey About the SAP BTP Command Line Interface (btp CLI) – Your Opinion Matters! https://blogs.sap.com/2023/06/12/survey-about-the-sap-btp-command-line-interface-btp-cli-your-opinion-matters/  

SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Datasphere learning opportunities 

  • What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud in June 2023: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/06/09/you-are-invited-whats-new-in-sap-hana-cloud-in-june-2023/ 
  • Using the Optimizer for SQLScript Programming in SAP HANA: https://open.sap.com/courses/hanasql2 
  • Introduction to SAP Datasphere: https://open.sap.com/courses/dsp1 
  • Register for the Plan to Win: Get Active with Data Hackathon: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/06/12/5-reasons-to-register-for-the-plan-to-win-get-active-with-data-hackathon/ 

June Developer Challenge 

  • Announcement Blog Post: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/06/07/sap-ui5-web-components-for-react-challenge-table-mastery-and-custom-components/  
  • Week 3 Discussion Post: https://groups.community.sap.com/t5/application-development/sap-developer-challenge-ui5-web-components-for-react-challenge/m-p/267497#M1757  

Python Package for Large Language Models for AI Core 

  • Blog post: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/06/08/harnessing-the-power-of-large-language-models-empowering-sap-btp-application-developers/ 
  • Python package: https://pypi.org/project/sap-ai-core-llm/ 

Kyma Updates from India 

  • Kyma certification live: 
    https://learning.sap.com/certification/sap-certified-development-associate-side-by-side-extensibility-based-on-sap-btp-kyma-runtime?userlogin=true&book_certification=true 
  • SAP BTP, Kyma runtime Istio based JWT handler – sneak peek | SAP Blogs https://blogs.sap.com/2023/05/29/sap-btp-kyma-runtime-istio-based-jwt-handler-sneak-peek/ 
  • SAP BTP, Kyma runtime ORY Hydra OAuth2 Client migration https://blogs.sap.com/2023/06/06/sap-btp-kyma-runtime-ory-hydra-oauth2-client-migration/ 
  • Service Accounts easy with SAP BTP, Kyma runtime https://blogs.sap.com/2023/06/01/service-accounts-easy-with-sap-btp-kyma-runtime/ 
  • SaaS Self-Onboarding and One-Domain Concept in SAP BTP, Kyma Runtime using CAP https://blogs.sap.com/2023/06/01/saas-self-onboarding-and-one-domain-concept-in-sap-btp-kyma-runtime-using-cap/comment-page-1/  
  • Martin’s github repository https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-kyma-cap-multitenant-susaas 

 

================================================================================ 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 Survey About the SAP BTP Command Line Interface (btp CLI) 

0:46 SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Datasphere learning opportunities 

1:57 June Developer Challenge 

2:50 Python Package for Large Language Models for AI Core 

3:37 Kyma Updates from India 

6:28 Outro

entry.title: 🇩🇪 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Cologne, DE)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud Development Model in SAP S/4HANA 2022.  

We will give you an introduction about the ABAP Cloud Development in an SAP S/4HANA 2022 on-premise environment. The fundamental principle is that ABAP cloud-ready and ABAP classic code can coexist in the same system since the ABAP language version is defined on ABAP object level. This offers a gradual transition from the classic to the cloud extensibility model.

To understand the basic idea of ABAP Cloud Development you will learn how to:

- Use the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) to develop transactional Fiori applications

- Use public SAP APIs released by SAP to access SAP functionality and SAP data

- Use public SAP extension points released by SAP to extend SAP objects

- Use ADT (ABAP Development Tools) as your ABAP IDE

With the SAP S/4HANA 2022 release the same extensibility model can now be used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday 19 October 2023 in Cologne, Germany from 9:00 - 16:00 CET. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Address: Neomatic AG Offices,  Aachener Str. 23 50674 Cologne Germany

The instructor is Ingo Braeuninger

The event is kindly being hosted by Neomatic, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. 

Please find the prerequisites for this event here.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: 🇪🇸 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Madrid, Spain)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud Development Model in SAP S/4HANA 2022.  

We will give you an introduction about the ABAP Cloud Development in an SAP S/4HANA 2022 on-premise environment. The fundamental principle is that ABAP cloud-ready and ABAP classic code can coexist in the same system since the ABAP language version is defined on ABAP object level. This offers a gradual transition from the classic to the cloud extensibility model.

To understand the basic idea of ABAP Cloud Development you will learn how to:

- Use the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) to develop transactional Fiori applications

- Use public SAP APIs released by SAP to access SAP functionality and SAP data

- Use public SAP extension points released by SAP to extend SAP objects

- Use ADT (ABAP Development Tools) as your ABAP IDE

entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud Development Model in SAP S/4HANA 2022.  

We will give you an introduction about the ABAP Cloud Development in an SAP S/4HANA 2022 on-premise environment. The fundamental principle is that ABAP cloud-ready and ABAP classic code can coexist in the same system since the ABAP language version is defined on ABAP object level. This offers a gradual transition from the classic to the cloud extensibility model.

To understand the basic idea of ABAP Cloud Development you will learn how to:

- Use the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) to develop transactional Fiori applications

- Use public SAP APIs released by SAP to access SAP functionality and SAP data

- Use public SAP extension points released by SAP to extend SAP objects

- Use ADT (ABAP Development Tools) as your ABAP IDE

With the SAP S/4HANA 2022 release the same extensibility model can now be used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday 8 December 2023 in Mannheim, Germany from 9:00 - 16:00 CET. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Address: 

Brandeis Consulting

Mafinex Technologiezentrum

Julius-Hatry Str. 1

68163 Mannheim

The instructor is Andre Fischer

The event is kindly being hosted by Brandeis Consulting GmbH, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. 

Please find the prerequisites for this event here.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: My Top SAP TechEd Session Picks in 2023
entry.content:
November is just around the corner and while Devtoberfest is slowly coming to an end, the next great event is already on the horizon: SAP TechEd! This year, the annual gathering will take place on November 2 and 3, offering developers the opportunity to explore innovations in application development, generative AI, and much more. There are plenty of options to be a part of this exciting event. Whether you choose to attend SAP TechEd in Bangalore or participate virtually, be sure to sign up today to be among the first to learn about the latest innovations!  

To help you navigate the event, I've curated a list of my top three picks for onsite sessions at SAP TechEd Bangalore, with a heavy focus on the ABAP developer of course. If you're attending the immersive, fun, and educational experience in India, make sure to connect directly with SAP experts, industry influencers, and our vast developer community onsite. Additionally, don't miss out on these exciting sessions: 

  1. AD164 - Introduction of ABAP Cloud for Use by Classic ABAP Developers: This session is a must-attend for any developer coming from the classic ABAP world, and looking to upskill to the new ABAP Cloud development model. 
  2. AD260 - Building SAP Fiori Apps with ABAP Cloud on SAP BTP, ABAP Environment: This session is very close to my heart as it covers my favorite topics, ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming model (RAP).  Join this session to get familiar with RAP in general, as well as specific features like late numbering and how to leverage business events in your RAP Business Objects.  
  3. AD267 - Extending with ABAP Cloud and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model: Application extensibility is a super important topic to SAP and to our customers and partners, whether it be on-stack or side-by-side.  Join this hands-on session to get a sense of how we can extend our applications using both ABAP Cloud and RAP and the SAP Cloud Application Programming model (CAP).  

And if you want to get a little taste of many developer topics in one place, make sure to visit the Developer Garage on the show floor where we will have the App Space running. Attendees can complete various tutorials and earn free swag! Who doesn’t love swag?! Plus you may get to meet one of your favorite SAP Developer Advocates who might be hanging around, so check it out!  

For those opting for SAP TechEd Virtual this year, I have some great recommendations that you shouldn't miss: 

  1. DT103V - Extensibility with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition & DT200v – Extensibility with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition: As I mentioned, extensibility is critical to the success of our products, and the options for extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition are a little different than the options we have in the public edition, so we’ve put together two separate sessions to cover both scenarios. In these sessions, you will learn how we can leverage ABAP Cloud to create our extensions quickly and easily and of course following clean core principles.    
  2. KEY200 – Developer Keynote But let’s not forget about the Developer Keynote! So, a little bit of self-promotion never hurts right? Seriously folks, you are not going to want to miss this session. Our Developer Advocates team will be showing off some of the latest and greatest innovations happening at SAP, celebrating the 20th anniversary of the SAP Community with a fun storyline, as well as announcing the winners of our 4th annual Devtoberfest event which has just wrapped. Whether you are on-site in Bangalore or watching from home, it will be an awesome show for sure! 

If you're unable to make it to Bangalore but still want to experience the event with others, join our dedicated "watch-and-code" meetups. These half-day, in-person events will be held in 11 different locations worldwide, fostering collaboration, knowledge sharing, and engagement. It's a fantastic opportunity to come together, network, learn, and code!  

Before the event kicks off, I recommend visiting the SAP TechEd Learning zone where you can find tailored learning resources for all the hottest SAP TechEd topics and tracks. My favourite right now is the Application Development and Automation track where you can find most of the relevant topics for pro-code developers. So go check it out! 

One last thing: Don’t forget to claim your exclusive 25% discount on your next SAP Certification exam purchase – SAP’s giving them away for every SAP TechEd registrant, so make sure to be one of them! 

See you in Bangalore, or virtually on-line!

entry.title: 🇫🇷 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Strasbourg, FR)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud Development Model in SAP S/4HANA 2022.  

We will give you an introduction about the ABAP Cloud Development in an SAP S/4HANA 2022 on-premise environment. The fundamental principle is that ABAP cloud-ready and ABAP classic code can coexist in the same system since the ABAP language version is defined on ABAP object level. This offers a gradual transition from the classic to the cloud extensibility model.

To understand the basic idea of ABAP Cloud Development you will learn how to:

- Use the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) to develop transactional Fiori applications

- Use public SAP APIs released by SAP to access SAP functionality and SAP data

- Use public SAP extension points released by SAP to extend SAP objects

- Use ADT (ABAP Development Tools) as your ABAP IDE

With the SAP S/4HANA 2022 release the same extensibility model can now be used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday 8 December 2023 in Strasbourg, France from 9:00 - 16:00 CET. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Address:   

Nagarro France 

8a Rue Icare, 67960 Entzheim

The instructor is Ingo Braeuninger

The event is kindly being hosted by Nagarro, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. 

Please find the prerequisites for this event here.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud Development Model in SAP S/4HANA 2023.  

We will give you an introduction about the ABAP Cloud Development in an SAP S/4HANA 2023 on-premise environment. The fundamental principle is that ABAP cloud-ready and ABAP classic code can coexist in the same system since the ABAP language version is defined on ABAP object level. This offers a gradual transition from the classic to the cloud extensibility model.

To understand the basic idea of ABAP Cloud Development you will learn how to:

- Use the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) to develop transactional Fiori applications

- Use public SAP APIs released by SAP to access SAP functionality and SAP data

- Use public SAP extension points released by SAP to extend SAP objects

- Use ADT (ABAP Development Tools) as your ABAP IDE

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 release the same extensibility model can now be used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday 22 Feb 2024 in Berlin, Germany from 9:00 - 16:00 CET. The language of the event and the content will be English.

entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud Development Model in SAP S/4HANA 2023.  

We will give you an introduction about the ABAP Cloud Development in an SAP S/4HANA 2023 on-premise environment. The fundamental principle is that ABAP cloud-ready and ABAP classic code can coexist in the same system since the ABAP language version is defined on ABAP object level. This offers a gradual transition from the classic to the cloud extensibility model.

To understand the basic idea of ABAP Cloud Development you will learn how to:

- Use the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) to develop transactional Fiori applications

- Use public SAP APIs released by SAP to access SAP functionality and SAP data

- Use public SAP extension points released by SAP to extend SAP objects

- Use ADT (ABAP Development Tools) as your ABAP IDE

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 release the same extensibility model can now be used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, February 15th, 2024
entry.content:
February Developer Challenge on CAP Plugins – Week 2 

  • Week 2 task description and submission area: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/february-developer-challenge-cap-plugins-week-2/td-p/13605065 

'Did You Know' shorts Nr. 12 – Accessing SAP HANA Cloud instances with the hana-cli tool 

  • SAP HANA Developer Command Line Interface: https://github.com/SAP-samples/hana-developer-cli-tool-example 

Release Navigator for SAP BTP What’s New in Q4 2023 and What’s Next in 2024 

  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/release-navigator-for-sap-btp-what-s-new-in-q4-2023-and-what-s-next-in-2024/ba-p/13593034 

Developer Insights Survey 

  • 2024’s SAP Developer Insights Survey is ready for you (blog post): https://blogs.sap.com/2024/01/15/2024s-sap-developer-insights-survey-is-ready-for-you/ 

What’s New with SAP Build Process Automation: January 2024 

  • What's new blogpost by Jesse, Product Expert: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/what-s-new-with-sap-build-process-automation-january-2024/ba-p/13586989 
  • Robin Kiwitt video on new layout and usability improvements in SAP Build Process Automation: https://youtu.be/R1TP8i8AvFs 
  • New tutorials on the new Process Editor layout: https://developers.sap.com/mission.sap-process-editor-layout-usability-improvements.html,  https://developers.sap.com/group.boost-process-automation-layout.html 
  • Environments Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/environments 
  • SAP Build Content in the SAP Accelerator Hub: https://api.sap.com/build 
  • New options in the Enhanced Recorder: https://help.sap.com/docs/intelligent-robotic-process-automation/cloud-studio-user-guide/different-types-of-set-activity-options-in-enhanced-recorder 

 ===================================================================== 

CHAPTER TITLES 

0:00 Intro 

0:10 February Developer Challenge on CAP Plugins – Week 2 

1:15 'Did You Know' shorts Nr. 12 – Accessing SAP HANA Cloud instances with the hana-cli tool 

1:47 Release Navigator for SAP BTP What’s New in Q4 2023 and What’s Next in 2024 

3:10 Developer Insights Survey 

3:53 What’s New with SAP Build Process Automation: January 2024 

TRANSCRIPT

[DJ]As you may know, the Developer Advocates are running another Developer Challenge this month and this time it's all about CAP plugins, which are great. So this week sees the second task in the challenge, which introduces another CAP plugin, this time for GraphQL. Now, while many of you already realize that CAP is absolutely awesome for rapidly building OData services and much more, CAP itself is actually a lot more protocol and database agnostic than you might think. So yes, if GraphQL is your bag, then this plugin that you're gonna be learning about on this task is for you. Anyway, if you haven't already completed task one of this developer challenge, Well, first of all, why not? But second of all, just head back with the link, complete task one, then come back and do this task, task two on the CAP plugin for GraphQL. It's fun and you learn stuff. What's not to like?

[Thomas]Did you know that the HANA CLI has a command list information about all your HANA Cloud instances? It contains information like memory, sizing, and quick links to help you navigate to the cockpit, HANA Cloud Central, or the Database Explorer.

[Shilpa]Hey everyone, you probably have heard of Release Navigator for SAP Business Technology Platform. If you want to get consolidated release information across SAP BTP products and services, then bookmark this page. Not just that, you can find blog posts, newsletters, videos, podcasts and more helpful content. Let me highlight some of the latest features. Firstly, based on your feedback, there is another way to access Release Navigator from SAP Support Portal. Click on Offerings and Programs and select Release Ready. Second, you can find the quarterly maintenance window according to the latest releases for the products featured. Additionally, some improvements in the usability. You can get to the product pages with less clicks now due to a simplified navigation on the home page of the Release Navigator. And lastly, Release Navigator is created using SAP Enable Now, in which you can translate all text on the Navigator pages automatically to 40 languages. The upcoming updates for the Release Navigator are scheduled on a quarterly basis in alignment with the harmonized release calendar for SAP Cloud products. You can refer to the blog post by Chris for further information and provide your feedback. Thank you.

[Riley]Howdy, developers. We're four weeks into the SAP Developer Insights Survey. We've started to look at the data and we're seeing some interesting signals there. But we're not finished. If you haven't taken the survey yet, please click on the link below and give us your feedback. If you started the survey but somehow got distracted, you can click on the same link and pick up where you left off. More samples help us have a better confidence in the data that we're actually seeing. So every little bit helps. People tell us it takes about 10 minutes to complete the survey, so it shouldn't be too much of your time. We appreciate your help and we will be back in about two weeks after the end of the survey on March 1st to give you an external report on what we're seeing. See you then.

[Srini]Dear SAP Builders, today I want to share with you highlights of some of the awesome features and enhancements that were released in December 2023 and January 2024 in SAP Build Process Automation. First up, we have the Process Editor, which now has a vertical layout and some great usability improvements. Check out this video by Robin Quid, product manager of SAP Build Process Automation. We also have some new tutorials on this topic. So make sure to try them out. We have introduced a new governance capability called environments. Environments provide a virtual space where you can deploy and run projects with an ability to create different shared environments. If you are curious to learn how to use this new capability, head over to this help portal page. In December, 2023, we added 19 new content packages. These packages are tailored for different lines of businesses, such as finance, procurement, IT, sales, and more. To see all the available packages, I recommend you exploring SAP Build in the SAP Accelerator Hub. There is now a new enhanced recorder in Desktop Agent 3 with various options, providing more control over the recording process with an user-friendly experience to manage your automation activities. Another exciting addition is the Run Now action for event triggers. This feature enables you to test your end-to-end processes locally with expected payload format without having to connect to multiple systems. And here is some great news for those who love customization. You can now build API actions from scratch using the Action Editor without relying on existing templates. It is super easy to use. Just follow the steps mentioned in this blog post by our product expert, Jesse. If you have any feedback on these enhancements, Please let us know in the comments of this blog post. Happy automating.

entry.title: 🇩🇪 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Freiburg, DE)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud Development Model in SAP S/4HANA 2023.  

We will give you an introduction about the ABAP Cloud Development in an SAP S/4HANA 2023 on-premise environment. The fundamental principle is that ABAP cloud-ready and ABAP classic code can coexist in the same system since the ABAP language version is defined on ABAP object level. This offers a gradual transition from the classic to the cloud extensibility model.

To understand the basic idea of ABAP Cloud Development you will learn how to:

- Use the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) to develop transactional Fiori applications

- Use public SAP APIs released by SAP to access SAP functionality and SAP data

- Use public SAP extension points released by SAP to extend SAP objects

- Use ADT (ABAP Development Tools) as your ABAP IDE

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 release the same extensibility model can now be used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday 19 April 2024 in Freiburg, Germany from 9:00 - 16:00 CET. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Address:   Nagarro ES GmbH - Fahnenbergplatz 1 - 79098 Freiburg im Breisgau, Deutschland

The instructor is Andre Fischer

The event is kindly being hosted by Nagarro ES GmbH, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. 

Please find the prerequisites for this event here.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.
